input.command:
gh issue create --repo TheurgicDuke771/DataQ --title "admin webhook-config surface mislabels dbt connections as airflow (two-provider if/else missed by ADR 0029)" --label bug --milestone "v1.1 Week 2 — Portability: OTel logs, secrets lifecycle, dry-run depth" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

`admin_service.webhook_configs()` loops over `ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS` (now `("adf", "airflow", "dbt")` since #611/ADR 0029) but its body still branches `if provider == "adf": … else: # airflow`. A `dbt` connection falls into the else-branch and is emitted with:

- `provider="airflow"` (admin_service.py:231)
- `inbound_url=…/api/v1/orchestration/events/airflow` (:233) — wrong endpoint
- `signing_secret_name=settings.airflow_webhook_secret_name` (:235) — wrong secret name

The frontend admin page (`frontend/src/api/admin.ts:72` → `GET /admin/orchestration/webhooks`) surfaces these rows directly, so an admin configuring a dbt webhook gets guidance pointing at the Airflow endpoint and the Airflow signing key.

## Root cause

`webhook_configs` was written in #490/#493 when the tuple was two providers; the #611 migration widened the tuple + routes but didn't touch this function. `backend/tests/api/test_admin.py` covers adf and airflow rows but never creates a `dbt` connection, so nothing caught the fallthrough.

## Related gap (same fix PR can cover it)

`.env.app.example` documents `ADF_WEBHOOK_SECRET_NAME` / `AIRFLOW_WEBHOOK_SECRET_NAME` but not `DBT_WEBHOOK_SECRET_NAME` (config.py has the Python default `dbt-webhook-secret`, but the env template should list the knob like its siblings).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `webhook_configs()` dispatches per provider (no adf-vs-else fallthrough) — dbt rows carry `provider="dbt"`, the `/orchestration/events/dbt` inbound URL, and `settings.dbt_webhook_secret_name`
- [ ] Test creates a `dbt` connection and asserts its webhook-config row (and guards the next provider addition — parametrize over `ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS`)
- [ ] `DBT_WEBHOOK_SECRET_NAME` documented in `.env.app.example`

Found by the /code-review pass on #646 (cross-file tracer angle) and verified against source + git history.
EOF
)"

1	# dbt Core lineage project (`dataq_lineage`) — harness #609
2	
3	Self-hosted **dbt Core** project that builds multi-hop lineage objects on top of the
4	Flow-A retail base tables in Snowflake:
5	
6	```
7	DATAQ_DB.RETAIL.{ORDERS_HEADER, ORDER_LINES, CUSTOMERS, PRODUCTS}   ← base (Airflow/ADF loads)
8	        │  (source)
9	        ▼
10	DATAQ_DB.ANALYTICS_STG.{stg_orders, stg_order_lines, stg_customers, stg_products}   ← staging VIEWS
11	        │  (ref)
12	        ▼
13	DATAQ_DB.ANALYTICS.{mart_order_revenue, mart_customer_orders}   ← mart DYNAMIC TABLES (target_lag 1h)
14	```
15	
16	It exists to (a) exercise **dbt as a third orchestration layer** (the DataQ-side
17	provider is a separate issue, #611) and (b) be the **test bed for the G-d lineage
18	work** (#596 — `manifest.json` is the node-graph input). Harness-only, ADR 0021 —
19	**not** DataQ app code, **not** git-tracked.
20	
21	## Why this shape
22	- **Staging = views, marts = dynamic tables** → real base→staging→mart lineage, and
23	  the dynamic tables' refresh lag gives DataQ a **freshness monitor** to measure.
24	- **Self-hosted dbt Core**, host-agnostic on purpose: dbt Cloud free has no
25	  API/scheduler, dbt-on-Snowflake dies with the trial, Databricks-Jobs-dbt couples
26	  to the Jobs API. A plain container survives the cloud wind-down (#590/#591); the
27	  provider (#611) observes it purely through **artifacts + webhook**, never a
28	  host-specific API.
29	
30	## Authentication & privileges
31	- **dbt → Snowflake:** password auth, env-var driven (`profiles.yml` is 100%
32	  `env_var()`, no secret in the file). Same creds the Airflow loader uses.
33	- **Role = `DATAQ_LOADER`, not `ACCOUNTADMIN`.** dbt owns the two `ANALYTICS*`
34	  schemas (needs `CREATE SCHEMA ON DATABASE`, granted in `../terraform/snowflake.tf`
35	  → `loader_create_schema`) and grants `SELECT`/`USAGE` on them to `DATAQ_READER`
36	  (the on-run-end hooks) so a DataQ suite (running as the least-priv reader) can read
37	  the marts. Override with `DBT_SNOWFLAKE_ROLE` (defaults to `DATAQ_LOADER`; we use a
38	  dbt-specific var so `SNOWFLAKE_ROLE=ACCOUNTADMIN` in `secrets.sh` can't re-escalate).
39	- **Change tracking (dynamic-table prerequisite):** dynamic tables require change
40	  tracking on their base tables, and `DATAQ_LOADER` can't enable it (doesn't own
41	  them). `ORDERS_HEADER` + `CUSTOMERS` → `change_tracking = true` in `snowflake.tf`;
42	  `ORDER_LINES` + `PRODUCTS` (write_pandas-created) → run **`bootstrap_change_tracking.sql`**
43	  once as `ACCOUNTADMIN`. ⚠️ `write_pandas` overwrite `CREATE OR REPLACE`s the latter
44	  two and drops the setting — stable during the wind-down (loader stopped), re-run the
45	  bootstrap if `flow_a_snowflake_load` runs again.
46	- **dbt → ADLS (artifact upload):** `AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING` (same seam as
47	  mockdata's `AdlsStore`).
48	
49	## Hosting (one image, three homes)
50	| Context | How |
51	|---|---|
52	| Dev iteration | local venv/conda: `source ../secrets.sh && dbt build --profiles-dir .` |
53	| Azure window | ACA Job `dbt-lineage` on `dataq-cae` (`../terraform/dbt_job.tf`) |
54	| Post-wind-down | `docker compose -f docker-compose.yml run --rm dbt` |
55	
56	## Run it
57	
58	### Local (fastest dev loop — hits live Snowflake, not the Azure harness)
59	```bash
60	# one-time: change tracking on the write_pandas base tables (as ACCOUNTADMIN)
61	source ../secrets.sh
62	snowsql -a "$SNOWFLAKE_ACCOUNT" -u "$SNOWFLAKE_USER" -r ACCOUNTADMIN \
63	  -d DATAQ_DB -s RETAIL -f bootstrap_change_tracking.sql   # or paste into a worksheet
64	
65	# build (needs a local dbt-snowflake — see requirements.txt)
66	pip install -r requirements.txt
67	dbt deps            # no-op (no packages) but harmless
68	dbt build --profiles-dir .
69	# artifacts land in ./target/{manifest,run_results}.json
70	```
71	
72	### Container / ACA Job (the "runs from the container host" acceptance)
73	```bash
74	# build + push the image to the harness ACR (login server from terraform output)
75	az acr login -n <registry>
76	docker build -t <login_server>/dbt-lineage:latest .          # context = this dir
77	docker push <login_server>/dbt-lineage:latest
78	terraform -chdir=../terraform apply -target=azurerm_container_app_job.dbt_lineage
79	az containerapp job start -n dbt-lineage -g dataq-rg          # on-demand run
80	```
81	
82	## Artifacts (`#611` / `#596`)
83	`upload_artifacts.py` publishes `manifest.json` + `run_results.json` to
84	`adls://<landing-account>/raw/dbt/runs/<UTC-ts>/` and overwrites `.../raw/dbt/latest/`
85	(stable pointer for the #611 poller). No-op locally (`DBT_ARTIFACTS_URI` unset).
86	
87	## DataQ suite target
88	Point a DataQ Snowflake suite at `DATAQ_DB.ANALYTICS.MART_ORDER_REVENUE`
89	(checks on `line_count`/`reconciled_subtotal` + a **freshness** monitor on `ORDER_TS`)
90	to prove DataQ runs against non-base, dbt-produced dynamic tables.
91

170	}
171

176	    az datafactory trigger stop -g "$RG" --factory-name "$ADF" --name "$t" >/dev/null && log "  ADF trigger $t -> Stopped"

All done — everything merged. Here's the full picture:

## Merged (3 PRs, all squash-merged with green CI)

- **#646 — agent optimizations** (`qa-verifier` + `ui-tester`): the original work plus a `fixup:` commit addressing all nine doc findings from the /code-review pass (scenario renumbering, source-of-truth pointers for the provider list, the webhook-secret local prerequisite, the too-narrow `--label bug` dedup filter, the un-swept new routes, screenshot-lifecycle contradiction, `:connectionId` param name, and the false-precision thresholds).
- **#648 — ADR index single-sourced** (your suggestion): CLAUDE.md §9 is now a short pointer to [docs/adr/README.md](docs/adr/README.md) instead of a duplicate 29-row table (~9KB dropped from every session's context). The drift you suspected was real and live: the README still listed **ADR 0026 as Proposed/Pending** while the ADR file says Accepted — fixed as part of making the index the single source. The docs-consistency-guard agent's rule 4 was retargeted to check index-vs-ADR-file status agreement.
- **#649 — §12 harness row refreshed** for the dbt leg (below).

Also filed **[#647](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/647)** (bug, v1.1 W2) — a real backend bug the review's cross-file tracer found and verified: `admin_service.webhook_configs()` still has the pre-ADR-0029 two-provider `if adf / else airflow` branch, so **dbt connections are reported as airflow** with the wrong inbound URL and signing-secret name on the admin page. The issue carries file:line evidence, git-history root cause, and acceptance criteria.

## Harness script — your hunch was right

`harness_window.sh` had zero dbt awareness while the `dbt-lineage` ACA Job (built today, #609) is deployed with a live nightly cron. I updated the script (harness-side, `bash -n` clean): `run --dbt` fires the job manually (resume → start → wait → re-suspend; needs live Snowflake, like `--adf`, and runs last so marts refresh from a same-window load), and `status`/`stop` now cover the job so `stop` disarms its cron like the mockdata ones. A review agent verified the CLAUDE.md row against the actual script and terraform before #649 merged.

**One decision for you:** the `dbt-lineage` cron (`0 2 * * *` UTC) has been armed since the job was created today — I didn't suspend it unilaterally in case you want tonight's run for #611 artifact testing. Say the word and I'll suspend it now; otherwise the next `harness_window.sh stop` takes care of it.
